title: greppy hardware evidence artifact
description: One reproducible inference-evidence run of bench/hardware_evidence.sh on real hardware (beyond GitHub-hosted runners). Committed artifacts live under bench/hardware-evidence/ and MUST NOT contain anything host-identifying: no hostnames, no usernames, no serial numbers, no absolute paths. Hardware make/model, driver versions, and OS versions are deliberately recorded; they are the evidence. Model digests are recorded so evidence collected against a superseded model is mechanically detectable (compare against crates/cli/assets/MODEL_ASSETS.json).

created_utc.description: UTC timestamp of the run, ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).

os_version.description: Kernel release (Linux) or product version (macOS). Never the node name.

cpu_model.description: CPU brand string (/proc/cpuinfo model name, sysctl machdep.cpu.brand_string).

apple_silicon_generation.description: Apple Silicon generation parsed from the CPU brand string (e.g. "M5"). The Metal tensor-ops (Metal 4) inference path only lights up on generations/OS builds whose GPU exposes it; evidence collected on an older generation marks the tensor path as pending newer hardware in measurements.notes.

gpu.description: Discrete/integrated GPU used by the run; null for pure-CPU platforms.

metal_family.description: Metal feature family reported by the product's backend probe (e.g. metal4/tensor-ops vs metal3/simdgroup).

source_sha.description: Git commit the measured binary was built from (passed via --source-sha; "unknown" if the build is untracked).

backend_compiled.description: Whether the requested backend is compiled into this binary (registry probe `compiled`).

selected.description: Backend the product actually selected (doctor .inference.registry.selected_backend). Must equal `requested` for a passing artifact.

selected_device.description: Product device id (e.g. cuda:0) — an enumeration index, not host-identifying.

cpu_capabilities.description: SIMD capabilities the product's own CPU probe reports (avx2, avx-vnni, avx512f, neon, dotprod). Empty on x86 means the binary ran on its SIMD baseline fallbacks.

baseline_x86_required.description: True when the run was invoked with --require-baseline-x86: the harness additionally asserts the CPU lacks AVX-VNNI/AVX-512 so the passing checks prove the SIMD fallback paths.

cuda_toolkit_visible.description: CUDA runs only: whether a CUDA toolkit (nvcc) was reachable in the harness environment. Must be false for a valid runtime-only artifact — the shipped binary bundles its CUDA backend (nvcc-built with --cudart=static) and needs only the NVIDIA driver's libcuda.so.1 at runtime.

cuda_backend_needed_libs.description: CUDA runs only: DT_NEEDED entries of the materialized bundled backend library. Must not contain cudart/cublas/nvrtc.

models.description: Digests of the models embedded in the measured binary, as reported by `greppy doctor --json` (.inference.models). Compare against crates/cli/assets/MODEL_ASSETS.json to detect evidence that predates a model promotion.

tree_sha256.description: Canonical content digest of the generated fixture tree; identical across platforms for the same harness revision.

index_cold_ms.description: Wall-clock `greppy index` on the fixture from an empty store: parsing, graph build, embedding AND summary inference.

vram.description: CUDA runs only: nvidia-smi memory.used polling on the measured GPU for the whole harness run. peak-baseline bounds the product's VRAM footprint (other tenants may inflate both equally).

notes.description: Free-form caveats, e.g. shared GPU tenancy, or hardware paths this platform cannot prove yet (Metal tensor-ops needs an M5-class GPU; baseline-x86 needs a pre-AVX-VNNI machine).

status.description: pass iff every entry in `checks` passed. Only passing artifacts belong in bench/hardware-evidence/.

latency.description: Warm-daemon latency over `calls` sequential invocations (one unmeasured warm-up call precedes the series).
